description: CLI for Ring doorbells, cameras, chimes — devices, events, snapshots, recordings, motion, lights, siren

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Set up Ring credentials. Prompts for email, password, and a 6-digit 2FA code that Ring delivers via SMS/email at login time. The OAuth token (with refresh) is cached in the profile data directory and reused on subsequent runs. Use --force/-F to clear the cached token and re-prompt; use --profile/-p to save to a non-default profile.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Drop stored credentials and the cached OAuth token for the given profile (default: active profile).

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Report whether each profile has saved credentials and a usable Ring token. Add --table/-t for a human-readable table.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Force a refresh of the Ring OAuth access token using the stored refresh token. Useful when verifying the token-rotation path; normal commands refresh automatically.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Test authentication by hitting Ring with the cached token and counting visible devices. Pass --verbose for the full check set; --table for a human-readable summary.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all auth profiles for the ring CLI.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show the contents (credentials + token presence) of a single profile.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new auth profile to log a different Ring account into.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Mark the named profile as the active default for `ring auth login`.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete an auth profile and its cached token.

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage Ring auth profiles -- list, get, create, set-default, delete. Use to track multiple Ring accounts.

auth.usage_instructions: Ring authentication -- login (with 2FA prompt), logout, status, refresh. Supports multiple profiles via --profile/-p. Use `ring auth login --force` to clear the cached OAuth token and re-run the 2FA challenge.

list.help: List all Ring devices on the account.

options.name: --family
options.help: Restrict to a single device family (doorbots, authorized_doorbots, stickup_cams, chimes, other)

list.usage_instructions: Enumerate every device on the account across all families. Filter to a single family with --family (doorbots, stickup_cams, chimes, authorized_doorbots, other). Use --table/-t for a table; --filter/-f for field:op:value; --properties/-p to limit columns.

get.help: Get full details (including health) for a single device.
get.usage_instructions: Get full detail (including health, battery, wifi) for a single device. Accepts the device name (case-insensitive) or numeric ID.

health.help: Show battery, wifi, and connection status for a device.
health.usage_instructions: Battery, wifi signal, and connection status for a single device. Lighter weight than `devices get` -- skips capability introspection.

devices.usage_instructions: Manage Ring devices (doorbells, cameras, chimes, intercoms). `devices list` enumerates everything on the account; `devices get <name-or-id>` returns full detail including battery and wifi; `devices health <name-or-id>` returns connectivity only. Filter by family with --family (doorbots, stickup_cams, chimes, authorized_doorbots, other).

options.name: --device
options.help: Limit to a single device (name or numeric ID)

options.name: --kind
options.help: Filter by event kind (ding, motion, on_demand, alarm)

list.usage_instructions: List recent events (dings, motion, on-demand). Defaults to all video devices; restrict with --device/-d. Filter by event kind with --kind/-k (ding, motion, on_demand, alarm).

get.help: Look up a single event by ID under a specific device. Ring's history endpoint is per-device, so we scan the last 200 events on the given device and return the matching entry.

options.name: --device
options.help: Device name or numeric ID the event belongs to

get.usage_instructions: Look up a single event by ID under a specific device. Scans the last 200 events on that device for the matching ID.

arguments.name: event_id
arguments.help: Event ID to download. Omit with --recent to grab the last N events

options.name: --device
options.help: Device name or numeric ID the event belongs to

options.name: --recent
options.help: Download the most recent N events instead of a specific event_id

options.name: --kind
options.help: Restrict --recent to a single kind (ding, motion, ...)

download.usage_instructions: Download recordings as MP4 files. Either pass an EVENT_ID positional argument, OR pass --recent/-r N to grab the last N events. Output defaults to ~/Downloads/ring/ but can be overridden with --output/-o.

events.usage_instructions: Ring event history (dings, motion, on-demand). `events list` lists recent events across all video devices unless restricted with --device/-d; `events get EVENT_ID --device <name>` retrieves a specific event; `events download` saves MP4 recordings. Pass --recent N --device <name> to grab the last N recordings without first looking up an event id.

list.help: List devices that support snapshot capture (doorbells and cameras).
list.usage_instructions: List devices that support snapshot capture (doorbells and cameras).

get.help: Trigger a fresh snapshot capture and save it locally.
get.usage_instructions: Trigger a fresh JPEG capture from a doorbell or camera and save it locally. Defaults to ~/Downloads/ring/<device>_snapshot.jpg; override with --output/-o.

snapshot.usage_instructions: Capture snapshots from doorbells and cameras. `snapshot list` shows snapshot-capable devices; `snapshot get --device <name>` triggers a fresh JPEG capture (defaults to ~/Downloads/ring/<device>_snapshot.jpg unless --output is given).

enable.help: Enable motion detection on a doorbell or camera.
enable.usage_instructions: Turn motion detection ON for a doorbell or camera.

disable.help: Disable motion detection on a doorbell or camera.
disable.usage_instructions: Turn motion detection OFF for a doorbell or camera.

status.help: Show whether motion detection is currently enabled.
status.usage_instructions: Show whether motion detection is currently enabled for a device.

motion.usage_instructions: Toggle motion detection per device. Use `motion status --device <name>` to inspect, `motion enable` / `motion disable` to change. Motion zones and sensitivity sliders are not exposed by the ring-doorbell library; edit them in the Ring mobile app.

on.usage_instructions: Turn the floodlights on for a Ring stickup_cam with lights. Fails if the device does not have lights (e.g. doorbell, chime).

off.usage_instructions: Turn the floodlights off for a Ring stickup_cam with lights.

lights.usage_instructions: Control floodlights on Ring stickup_cams. `lights on --device <name>` and `lights off --device <name>`. Fails with a clear error if used against a device that does not have lights (e.g. a doorbell or chime).

options.name: --duration
options.help: Siren duration in seconds (Ring enforces a max around 300s)

on.usage_instructions: Trigger the siren for --duration seconds (default 30). Stickup_cams only.

off.help: Silence an active siren by setting its remaining duration to 0.
off.usage_instructions: Silence an active siren. Stickup_cams only.

siren.usage_instructions: Sound or silence the siren on Ring stickup_cams. `siren on --device <name> --duration 30` triggers it for N seconds (default 30); `siren off --device <name>` silences it. Ring enforces an upper bound around 300s server-side.

test.help: Play a test sound on a chime.

options.name: --device
options.help: Chime device name or numeric ID

test.usage_instructions: Play a test sound on a Ring chime device. Use --kind ding for the doorbell tone, --kind motion for the motion alert tone.

chime.usage_instructions: Play test sounds on Ring chime devices. `chime test --device <name> --kind ding|motion` plays the selected test sound on the chime speaker.

set.usage_instructions: Set the device speaker volume to --value (0-10 inclusive).

volume.usage_instructions: Set device volume (0-10). `volume set --device <name> --value <0-10>` -- applies to doorbell speakers, chime speakers, and intercom speakers.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached responses for this CLI.

cache.usage_instructions: Local response cache. `cache clear` removes every cached response. Use the global --no-cache flag on any command to bypass the cache for a single call.

usage_instructions: Use the `ring` CLI for ALL Ring doorbell, camera, chime, and intercom operations on Adam's Ring account. The CLI wraps the ring-doorbell Python library (same library that powers the Home Assistant integration). First-time use requires `ring auth login` which prompts for email, password, and a 6-digit 2FA code Ring delivers via SMS/email; subsequent runs use the cached OAuth refresh token. Pass --table/-t on any read command for a human-readable table; default output is JSON on stdout. Device identifiers in commands accept either the case-insensitive device name (e.g. 'Front Door') or the numeric device ID. OUT OF SCOPE: Ring Alarm arm/disarm and motion-zone editing -- these are not exposed by the ring-doorbell library.
